title: Queue-history handoff and roadmap truth lint
goal: Make replan proposal history and roadmap truth mechanically visible: handoffs/reports include queue revisions and roadmap status cannot contradict slice/run evidence without a lint failure.
acceptance: Final reports, implementation summaries, and handoff JSON include a plan_revisions or equivalent queue-history section covering pending, accepted, rejected, deferred, and superseded proposals relevant to the run.

Accepted revisions are attested with evidence, authorizer, applied-at checkpoint, and before/after queue or slice-change summary; rejected/deferred proposals remain visible with rationale and revisit condition where applicable.
Unresolved pending proposals block handoff readiness unless the operator explicitly marks them non-blocking through the approved proposal/disposition mechanism.
A roadmap/matrix truth lint or generator checks that roadmap statuses cannot claim acceptance/closure contradicting .workflow slice JSON and named daemon run evidence.
The lint catches a fixture where a matrix row says done while the corresponding slice remains open or lacks completed-run closure evidence.
The implementation does not make roadmap Markdown authoritative; Markdown remains a summary over slice/run evidence or a checked planning note.
must_ask_if: The implementation would make docs/roadmap Markdown the source of truth instead of checking it against slice JSON and daemon/run evidence.
The implementation would silently suppress pending replan proposals from handoff/report output.
The implementation would auto-close slices or rewrite roadmap state without daemon completion evidence or operator-approved revision records.
The implementation would add a rich planning UI instead of the bounded lint/report/handoff surfaces declared by this slice.
verify: bash -lc 'if [ -x scripts/roadmap-truth-check ]; then scripts/roadmap-truth-check; elif [ -x scripts/roadmap_truth_check.py ]; then scripts/roadmap_truth_check.py; else cargo test roadmap_truth --quiet; fi'
